headlines.title: Obama says U.S. may be ‘worse off’ now than before Iran war
headlines.description: Former President Barack Obama said it seems like the United States has either returned to the status quo or is “worse off” now than before President Donald Trump launched the war on Iran in February.“We’ve now fought a war, spent billions and billions of dollars, you know, put enormous strain on our military.

headlines.title: Maryland Senate president faces primary after snubbing Democratic redistricting effort
headlines.description: Maryland state Senate president Bill Ferguson is facing a primary challenge in the wake of his decision last year not to pursue a new Democratic gerrymander in the state, in a rebuke to national Democrats and the governor.

headlines.title: Hannah Pingree, Bobby Charles advance to general election in Maine governor’s race
headlines.description: Maine’s major primaries were resolved early Friday morning after the state tabulated its ranked choice votes: Democrat Hannah Pingree will face off against Republican Bobby Charles in the race for governor, while Democrat Matt Dunlap will advance to the general election in the pivotal 2nd Congressional District race, NBC News projects

headlines.title: Americans see improved race relations since 2020, but divisions remain
headlines.description: Americans say race relations in the U.S. have improved significantly since the summer of 2020, amid protests calling for racial justice and police accountability after George Floyd’s death.

headlines.title: Obama Shares Vision for His New Chicago Presidential Center
headlines.description: In a wide-ranging interview, former President Barack Obama sat down for a conversation with TODAY’s Craig Melvin where he opens up about the inspiration behind his new Presidential Center in Chicago and weighs in on Iran, the nation’s political polarization, the splintering of media and more. He also discusses Michelle Obama’s legacy and her impact as first lady of the United States, his love of sports and basketball on display at the center, as well as the exhibit of letters he received w

headlines.title: First Algae, Now the Reflecting Pool's New Paint is Peeling Away
headlines.description: Already plagued by a massive algae bloom that turned its water green, the newly renovated Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool faces a new setback. Workers are now grappling with the landmark's new "American flag blue" paint peeling away just days after a costly $14.6 million upgrade. NBC’s Joe Fryer reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: US-Iran Talks Postponed as JD Vance Cancels Trip to Switzerland
headlines.description: Vice President JD Vance canceled plans to travel to Switzerland to meet with Iranian negotiators for the next round of peace talks. The abrupt cancellation comes after President Donald Trump signed a preliminary deal that could end the war. NBC’s Gabe Gutierrez reports for TODAY.

headlines.title: U.S. deportation tracker: Counting arrests, detentions and border crossings
headlines.description: President Donald Trump came into office promising the largest mass deportation in U.S history, targeting the more than 10 million unauthorized migrants living in the United States.

headlines.title: State Assemblyman Alex Bores speaks on AI and politics
headlines.description: State Assemblyman Alex Bores, a candidate for New York's 12th Congressional District, speaks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about why some top artificial intelligence companies are opposing his candidacy and whether AI should play a role in politics.

headlines.title: Map: How much you pay in federal and state gas taxes
headlines.description: As national gas price averages head toward $5 per gallon, one state has already suspended its gas tax, and now President Donald Trump and his Cabinet are talking about doing the same.

headlines.title: JD Vance no longer traveling to Switzerland for Iran talks
headlines.description: The thorniest issues have been left for the next round of negotiations, with big concessions made in return for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz.

headlines.title: Rep. Tom Kean to return to Congress on June 30 after monthslong absence
headlines.description: The New Jersey Republican is up for re-election this November after missing more than 100 votes during his absence this spring.
